blockingIssues.description: ReviewRequest still exposes a single top-level `Rules TrustedRules` object and each `Change` lacks any per-file trusted-instruction field. Although `TrustedRules.ReviewForPath()` was added, `Assembler.Assemble()` never calls it when constructing each change, so downstream provider requests cannot know which directory-scoped REVIEW.md applies to which changed file. The implementation therefore does not satisfy the requirement to apply directory-scoped REVIEW.md per changed file/path instead of collapsing to a single run-wide winner.

`handleNoteEvent` still falls back to inserting the `hook_events` row before calling `commandProcessor.Execute` when the processor does not implement `TransactionalCommandProcessor`. If a non-transactional processor is configured and command execution fails after the insert, the delivery is permanently deduplicated while the command side effect is lost, which violates the feature requirement that persistence plus side effects remain atomic/retry-safe across failures.

`runtimeVersion()` returns a hard-coded `"go1.25.6"` string rather than reading the real runtime version. The checked-in `testdata/bench/runtime_evidence.json` therefore reports whatever constant is in source, not the environment that actually produced the benchmark evidence, which makes the required environment snapshot in VAL-PERF-001 unreliable and easy to falsify.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture note to reflect per-path directory-scoped REVIEW.md handling rather than a single global TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown value.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax go-reliability procedure for validator-only lint/evidence-refresh follow-ups so workers are not forced to invent failing tests when no behavioral change is intended.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback for worker session b3488ca2-ef1f-470c-ad49-c540bfc619f8 marks `followedProcedure=false` solely because no new tests were added for the one-line gosimple cleanup; the worker transcript shows the change was limited to internal/bench/bench_test.go and then validators were rerun.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that benchmark evidence and environment snapshots must capture actual runtime/tool versions from the executing environment, not hard-coded literals.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: internal/bench/bench_test.go:542 hard-codes `"go1.25.6"`; testdata/bench/runtime_evidence.json records `environment.go_version: "1.25.6"`; no matching guidance appears in mission AGENTS.md, .factory/library/architecture.md, or .factory/library/environment.md.
